title: DL-025-embeddings
author: AI 面试题库
abstract:
DL-025 ⭐⭐⭐ What is Embedding? Word Embeddings and Applications

 Question
What are embeddings in deep learning? Explain word embeddings and their applications.

 Answer

 What are Embeddings?
Embeddings are dense, continuous vector representations of discrete items (words, images, users, etc.). They capture semantic meaning and relationships in a lower-dimensional space.

 Why Embeddings?

One-hot encoding: [0, 0, 1, 0, 0, ...]  ← Sparse, no semantic meaning
Embedding:        ...

sections.title: Question
sections.content: What are embeddings in deep learning? Explain word embeddings and their applications.

sections.title: What are Embeddings?
sections.content: **Embeddings** are dense, continuous vector representations of discrete items (words, images, users, etc.). They capture semantic meaning and relationships in a lower-dimensional space.

sections.title: Why Embeddings?
sections.content:
```
One-hot encoding: [0, 0, 1, 0, 0, ...]  ← Sparse, no semantic meaning
Embedding:        [0.2, -0.5, 0.8, ...] ← Dense, captures meaning
```

---

sections.title: 1. Word2Vec
sections.content:
```
Two architectures:
- CBOW: Predict word from context
- Skip-gram: Predict context from word

Properties:
- Captures semantic similarity
- Vector arithmetic: king - man + woman = queen
```

sections.title: 2. GloVe (Global Vectors)
sections.content:
```
Based on word co-occurrence matrix
Combines global matrix factorization with local context
```

sections.title: 3. FastText
sections.content:
```
Extends Word2Vec with subword information
Handles out-of-vocabulary words
Good for morphologically rich languages
```

sections.title: 4. Contextual Embeddings (Modern)
sections.content:
```
- ELMo: Context-dependent embeddings
- BERT: Bidirectional transformer embeddings
- Different embedding for same word in different contexts
```

---

sections.title: Semantic Similarity
sections.content:
```
Similar words have similar vectors:
cosine_similarity("cat", "dog") > cosine_similarity("cat", "car")
```

sections.title: Analogies
sections.content:
```
Vector arithmetic captures relationships:
vec("Paris") - vec("France") + vec("Germany") ≈ vec("Berlin")
```

sections.title: Dimensionality
sections.content:
```
Typical: 50-300 dimensions
Much smaller than vocabulary size (10,000+)
```

---

sections.title: 1. NLP Tasks
sections.content:
```
- Text classification
- Sentiment analysis
- Machine translation
- Named entity recognition
```

sections.title: 2. Recommendation Systems
sections.content:
```
- User embeddings
- Item embeddings
- Collaborative filtering
```

sections.title: 3. Graph Analysis
sections.content:
```
- Node embeddings (Node2Vec)
- Link prediction
- Community detection
```

sections.title: 4. Other Domains
sections.content:
```
- Image embeddings (CNN features)
- Audio embeddings
- User behavior embeddings
```

---

sections.title: Code Example
sections.content:
```python
from gensim.models import Word2Vec

# Train Word2Vec
sentences = [["cat", "sat", "on", "mat"],
             ["dog", "ran", "in", "park"]]
model = Word2Vec(sentences, vector_size=100, window=5, min_count=1)

# Get embedding
vector = model.wv["cat"]

# Find similar words
similar = model.wv.most_similar("cat", topn=5)
```

sections.title: Key Takeaways
sections.content:
- Embeddings: Dense vector representations
- Word2Vec, GloVe, FastText: Classic word embeddings
- BERT: Contextual embeddings (modern)
- Capture semantic meaning and relationships
- Applications: NLP, recommendations, graphs
